title: picea excelsa subsp. alpestris
intro: Picea excelsa subsp. alpestris, also known as the Scots Pine, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The Scots Pine has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Picea excelsa subsp. alpestris include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of the Scots Pine include essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
